summary: Frontend-only testing for Tazakkur Islamic app. Fixed SSR crash (window is not defined) in supabase.ts. Welcome screen loads correctly with all elements. Auth is blocked because anonymous sign-ins are disabled in Supabase. Could not test authenticated screens (tabs, onboarding, lessons, prayers, etc.).

integration_issues.issue: Supabase returns 'Anonymous sign-ins are disabled' (HTTP 422). Anonymous auth must be enabled in Supabase dashboard: Authentication > Settings > Allow anonymous sign-ins
prayers.tsx imports AsyncStorage directly and uses it in loadPrayerTimes(). This could cause SSR issues if prayer screen is pre-rendered. Same fix as supabase.ts may be needed.

action_items: CRITICAL: Enable anonymous sign-ins in Supabase dashboard -> Project: kmhhazpyalpjwspjxzry -> Authentication -> Settings -> Enable 'Allow anonymous sign-ins'. Without this, the entire app is non-functional.

FIXED (by T1): supabase.ts was crashing in SSR with 'window is not defined' error. Replaced AsyncStorage with safeStorage that uses localStorage on browser and no-op on SSR. This allowed the web bundler to complete.
Consider: prayers.tsx and potentially other tabs that use AsyncStorage directly in component body - should use useEffect wrapper to avoid potential SSR issues.
critical_code_review_comments: supabase.ts was using AsyncStorage as auth storage adapter which crashes Node.js SSR (window is not defined). Fixed by T1 with SSR-safe safeStorage implementation.
prayers.tsx uses AsyncStorage.getItem/setItem for prayer time caching - called inside useEffect so SSR-safe, but worth noting if server-side prefetch is added.
_layout.tsx uses AsyncStorage.getItem for city - inside useEffect, safe from SSR.
Anonymous Supabase auth is the only auth method. If it remains disabled, the entire app cannot be used.

success_rate.frontend: 20% - Welcome screen renders correctly, but auth flow fails (Supabase anonymous sign-in disabled)

context_for_next_testing_agent: T1 fixed the SSR crash in supabase.ts (window is not defined). The app now loads and bundles successfully on web. Critical blocker: Supabase anonymous sign-ins must be enabled in the project dashboard before any authentication-dependent UI can be tested. Once auth is working, test: onboarding (3 slides), 5 tabs navigation, lessons with course cards, prayers tab with city selector, hadiths 3-tab layout, rating leaderboard, settings with admin password.
rca_of_issue: Root cause 1 (FIXED): supabase.ts imported AsyncStorage from @react-native-async-storage/async-storage and passed it as Supabase auth storage adapter. During Expo web SSR (server-side rendering with 'output: static'), the code runs in Node.js where 'window' is not defined. AsyncStorage internally calls window.localStorage which throws ReferenceError. Fix: replaced AsyncStorage with safeStorage that checks typeof window !== 'undefined' before calling localStorage. Root cause 2 (OPEN): Supabase project has anonymous sign-ins disabled. Code calls supabase.auth.signInAnonymously() but Supabase returns 422 with 'Anonymous sign-ins are disabled'. Must enable this in Supabase dashboard.
